title: Engine test suite
description:
Create engine/tests/ as a comprehensive, AI-exemplary test suite for the engine daemon. This is the highest-priority test work: it validates existing code AND provides templates that AI assistants must follow when writing tests for new drivers and storage providers.

Directory layout:
  engine/tests/
    fixtures/
      wu-payload.json          # real/representative WU push payload
      ecowitt-payload.json     # real/representative Ecowitt push payload
      gw1000-response.json     # real/representative GW1000 LAN poll response
    domain/
      units.test.ts            # every Units.* conversion function, round-trip tests
      observation.test.ts      # Observation type shape, optional field handling
    ingest/
      normalizer-wu.test.ts    # normalizeWU(): timestamp always Date.now(), all fields mapped, SI units
      normalizer-ecowitt.test.ts
      normalizer-gw1000.test.ts
      normalizer-template.test.ts  # heavily-commented template: copy this when adding a new driver
    storage/
      adapter-contract.test.ts # Generic contract tests run against ANY StorageAdapter implementation
                               # (insert/query round-trip, insertReadings/latestReadings, aggregates, etc.)
                               # This is the template AI uses when writing tests for a new provider.
      mock-adapter.ts          # In-memory MockStorageAdapter satisfying full interface — used by
                               # all tests that need storage without a real DB
      sqlite.test.ts           # Runs adapter-contract tests against real SQLite (tmp file)
      mysql.test.ts            # Runs adapter-contract tests against MockStorageAdapter (no real server needed)
    api/
      router.test.ts           # Each REST endpoint: 200 paths, error paths, correct Content-Type
    almanac/
      calculator.test.ts       # Sunrise/sunset for known lat/lon/date, edge cases (polar day/night)

Key requirements:
- normalizer tests MUST assert: (a) timestamp is NOT the device-reported field, (b) temperature is in °C not °F, (c) wind speed is in m/s not mph, (d) rain is in mm not inches
- adapter-contract.test.ts uses a factory function createAdapter(): StorageAdapter so it is provider-agnostic
- normalizer-template.test.ts is exhaustively commented explaining what each assertion proves and why — it is a teaching document for AI
- Fixture JSON files are committed; no live hardware required
- engine/deno.json gets a test task: deno test --allow-read --allow-write --allow-env engine/tests/
- deno check engine/main.ts passes after all changes

title: Create shared/ package with canonical types and test fixtures
description:
Today engine/src/domain/observation.ts and web/lib/types.ts contain duplicate definitions of Observation, SensorReading, AggregateObservation, DailyAggregate, TodayStats, and SensorReading. There is already a TODO comment in web/lib/types.ts acknowledging this.

Create a shared/ package at the repo root that:
1. Exports all shared domain types (Observation, SensorReading, AggregateObservation, DailyAggregate, TodayStats, AlmanacData, SunTimes, MoonData) from shared/types.ts
2. Exports reusable test fixture factories from shared/testing/fixtures.ts — functions like makeObservation(overrides?), makeSensorReading(overrides?), makeWUPayload(), makeEcowittPayload() that return minimal valid instances. These are the building blocks for all engine and web tests.
3. Add shared/deno.json and register in root deno.json workspace
4. Update engine imports to use shared types
5. Update web/lib/types.ts to re-export from shared (keeping the web module as a stable import path for web code)

Acceptance criteria:
- No duplicate type definitions across engine and web
- deno check engine/main.ts and deno check web/main.ts both pass
- Fixture factories produce valid, type-safe instances
- AI assistants adding new observation fields only need to update shared/types.ts

title: Test Infrastructure
description:
Establish a comprehensive, AI-friendly test suite for both engine and web components of Zephyr. Tests serve two purposes: (1) validate existing code correctness, and (2) provide exemplary patterns so AI coding assistants can write tests for new contributions (drivers, storage providers, routes) that are complete, compliant, and correct.

Scope:
- engine/tests/ — primary focus; normalizer/driver tests, unit conversion, StorageAdapter contract, API endpoints, almanac
- web/tests/ — components and Preact signal state hooks
- shared/ — canonical shared types (Observation, SensorReading, AggregateObservation, etc. are duplicated between engine and web today) plus reusable test fixtures/factories
- docs/ai-assisted-development.md + AGENTS.md — updated to require tests for all AI-generated contributions

Design decisions:
- engine/tests/ and web/tests/ are independent; each sub-project can be type-checked and run in isolation
- shared/ lives at the repo root and is imported by both — eliminates the existing type duplication (see TODO in web/lib/types.ts)
- Storage provider tests use the real SQLite provider by default; MySQL and future providers use a mock StorageAdapter that satisfies the full interface contract
- Normalizer tests use fixture JSON payloads (committed to engine/tests/fixtures/) so tests are reproducible without live hardware
- Test files follow the naming convention <module>.test.ts alongside or in a parallel tests/ tree
- All test tasks must pass deno check before being considered done

title: Light/dark mode doesn't change automatically when system changes
Redesign config system from .env to structured config file
description: The current .env-based config (STATION_* env vars) cannot express structured data (multiple stations, arrays, nested objects) and is not standard for system services. Redesign to use a structured config file (TOML preferred for simplicity). Config file locations in priority order: /etc/zephyr/config.toml (production), ./config.toml (local dev), .env fallback for simple single-station dev setups. Config sections: stations (array of station definitions with id, name, lat, lon, altitude, timezone), storage (provider, sqlite_path or MySQL connection details), ingest (push enabled/disabled, poll enabled/disabled, device_registry mapping device push IDs to station IDs), api (port, host), web (port, engine_url). Update engine/config.ts to load and validate the new format. Maintain single-station backward compat (if one station defined, use as default).

title: [Epic] Configuration System Redesign
description: Replace .env-based configuration with a structured config file (TOML or YAML) that supports: multiple station definitions, standard Linux filesystem paths (/etc/zephyr/), typed and validated config, and works correctly in compiled binaries. Development mode should still support local .env or a local config file for convenience. This is a prerequisite for multi-station support.

title: Create GitHub Actions release workflow (tag-triggered, v*)
description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml triggered by tag push matching 'v*'. Only maintainers can push v* tags (GitHub repository Settings → Rules → Rulesets, Tag ruleset, pattern v*, restrict Create to maintainer users — this is the access control mechanism). Workflow steps: (1) checkout, (2) setup Deno, (3) build standalone binaries for linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, darwin-aarch64 via deno compile, (4) build .deb packages for linux-x86_64 and linux-aarch64 using nFPM or dpkg tooling, (5) create source tarball of the repo at the tagged commit, (6) create GitHub Release (using actions/create-release or gh cli) with all artifacts attached and auto-generated release notes from git log since last tag. Reference github-examples/release.yml in the repo for workflow patterns — note that example is far more complex (external storage, multi-repo); Zephyr only needs GitHub-hosted release artifacts.

title: Define standard filesystem paths for production deployment
description: Establish and document standard Linux filesystem paths for Zephyr in production, to be used consistently across systemd units, .deb package, install script, and config system: /etc/zephyr/ for config files (engine.env, web.env, or config.toml once redesigned), /var/lib/zephyr/ for data (zephyr.db and other persistent data), /usr/bin/zephyr-engine, /usr/bin/zephyr-web, /usr/bin/zephyr (CLI binary). A 'zephyr' system user owns /var/lib/zephyr/ and runs the daemons. These paths should be agreed upon as a design decision before implementing other deployment tasks so everything is consistent.

title: Create systemd unit files for engine and web daemons
description: Create systemd service unit files for both Zephyr daemons for deployment on Linux (Raspberry Pi OS, Debian, Ubuntu). Files: deploy/systemd/zephyr-engine.service and deploy/systemd/zephyr-web.service. Units should: run as a dedicated 'zephyr' system user, load config from /etc/zephyr/engine.env and /etc/zephyr/web.env as EnvironmentFile, set WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/zephyr, set Restart=on-failure with RestartSec=5, log to journald (no separate log file needed). Also create deploy/systemd/zephyr.target that wants and orders both services so 'systemctl enable zephyr.target' starts both. Document enable/start steps.

title: Fix web/main.ts to support custom PORT via Fresh .listen()
description: The current web/main.ts uses default Fresh startup which does not expose port configuration in the compiled binary. See https://github.com/denoland/fresh/issues/3548 and https://fresh.deno.dev/docs/concepts/app#listen for the fix. Rewrite web/main.ts to use app.listen({ port: Number(Deno.env.get('WEB_PORT') ?? 8081), hostname: Deno.env.get('WEB_HOST') ?? '0.0.0.0' }) so port is configurable at runtime via WEB_PORT env var. This is required for production deployments where default port conflicts. Must work in both dev (deno task dev) and compiled binary modes.

comments.author: Charlie Garrison
comments.text: Important: .listen() is only used when running your app directly with deno run -A main.ts. The default project setup uses deno task dev (Vite dev server) and deno task start (deno serve), which spawn their own servers - calling .listen() alongside these will create a second server and cause AddrInUse errors.

title: Add deno compile build tasks for engine and web daemons
description: Add deno task entries in root deno.json and per-package deno.json files to compile engine and web to standalone binaries using 'deno compile'. Engine: compile engine/main.ts with all required permissions (--allow-net, --allow-read, --allow-write, --allow-env). Web: run 'deno task build' first (Vite bundle of Fresh assets), then compile web/main.ts — the compiled web binary must include bundled static assets. Targets: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, darwin-aarch64. Output binaries to dist/ directory named zephyr-engine-{target} and zephyr-web-{target}. Add a root 'build:all' task that builds all targets.

title: [Epic] Build, Release & Deployment
description: Infrastructure for compiling Zephyr daemons and CLI to standalone binaries using deno compile, packaging as .deb and other distro formats, GitHub Actions release workflow triggered by protected tag push (v*), systemd unit files for Linux deployment, and an install one-liner script. Target platforms: linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64 (Raspberry Pi), darwin-aarch64.

title: Review all uses of `Date` API and switch to `Temporal`
description: Some usage of `Date` may be the correct choice, but probably all the uses should switch to the new Temporal API.

title: GitHub Actions CI workflow (test, type-check, lint, format)
description:
## Goal

Add a `.github/workflows/ci.yml` workflow that runs the full quality gate on every push and pull request. All checks that are required before submitting (see AGENTS.md §10) should be enforced automatically in CI.

## Triggers

- `push` — all branches
- `pull_request` — all branches targeting `main`
- Concurrency: cancel in-progress runs for the same branch/PR to avoid queue pile-ups

## Job: `ci` (ubuntu-latest)

Run all steps in a single job (no matrix needed at this stage).

### Steps

1. `actions/checkout@v4`
2. `denoland/setup-deno@v2` — pin to latest stable Deno release
3. Cache Deno deps (use `~/.deno` and `~/.cache/deno` as cache paths, keyed on `deno.json` + `engine/deno.json` + `web/deno.json` hashes)
4. **`deno task test:all`** — both engine and web test suites (78 + 33 steps)
5. **`deno check engine/main.ts`** — type-check engine
6. **`deno check web/main.ts`** — type-check web
7. **`deno lint`** — lint all source files
8. **`deno fmt --check`** — format check (note: `--check` flag, never the mutating form in CI)

### Step notes
- Each step should have a descriptive `name:` field so the GitHub UI is readable
- `deno task test:all` already depends on the `test:engine` and `test:web` sub-tasks — no need to run them separately
- `deno fmt --check` exits non-zero if any file would be reformatted — this is the correct CI behaviour
- `deno lint` and `deno fmt --check` are fast and should not be skipped even when only docs change; use a path filter only if CI becomes slow

## Notes

- The release workflow (`.github/workflows/release.yml`) already exists and handles binary compilation on tags — the new CI workflow is separate and must not interfere with it
- Do NOT run `deno compile` in CI (too slow; compilation is release-only)
- SQLite tests write to temp files — the `ubuntu-latest` runner has `/tmp` writable, no extra permissions needed
- MySQL tests run against `MockAdapter` (no real DB), so no service container is required
- The web test suite uses `happy-dom` (npm) — Deno will download it on first run; the dep cache step ensures subsequent runs are fast

## Acceptance Criteria

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` created and valid YAML
- All 8 steps named and ordered as above
- Workflow passes on a clean checkout of `main`
- PR status checks show individual step names in the GitHub UI

title: Update AI coding docs to require tests for all contributions
description:
The existing AI-assisted development documentation (docs/ai-assisted-development.md) and AGENTS.md cover how to write drivers, storage adapters, web routes, and themes — but say nothing about tests. This must be corrected: tests are now a first-class requirement.

Changes required:

1. AGENTS.md — Add a section (before 'Before Submitting') titled 'How to Write Tests for a New Contribution' that:
   - States that every new driver, storage provider, and web route MUST ship with tests
   - Points to the relevant template test file (e.g. engine/tests/ingest/normalizer-template.test.ts for drivers)
   - Lists the minimum assertions required for each contribution type
   - Adds deno test to the pre-submit checklist

2. docs/ai-assisted-development.md — For each prompt template (new push driver, LAN poll driver, storage adapter, web route):
   - Add a tests section to the prompt asking the AI to also write the test file
   - Add test-related items to the After Generation checklist
   - Update Post-Generation Checklist to include deno test

3. docs/ai-assisted-development.md — Add a new section 'Writing Tests' explaining:
   - Test directory structure (engine/tests/, web/tests/, shared/)
   - How to use fixture factories from shared/testing/fixtures.ts
   - How to use the MockStorageAdapter for storage-dependent tests
   - How to use normalizer-template.test.ts as a starting point
   - The three non-negotiable normalizer assertions (timestamp source, SI units)

Acceptance criteria:
- An AI assistant reading only AGENTS.md knows it must write tests and where to find the templates
- The prompt templates in ai-assisted-development.md produce test files when followed

title: Web test suite
description:
Create web/tests/ covering Preact signal state hooks and key UI components. DOM testing in Deno can require setup helpers — the repo already has test-examples/lib/ (test-env.ts, mock-websocket.ts) from a prior project as reference; evaluate whether those patterns apply and adapt as needed.

Directory layout:
  web/tests/
    setup.ts                  # DOM environment bootstrap (adapt test-examples/lib/ as needed)
    lib/
      test-env.ts             # setupTestEnvironment(), cleanupTestEnvironment()
      mock-fetch.ts           # mockEngineAPI() — stubs web/lib/api.ts fetch calls
    hooks/
      useObservationState.test.ts  # Signal reads/writes, update propagation, null initial state
    components/
      conditions/             # Render tests for each CurrentConditions sub-component
      layout/                 # Header, nav render tests
    islands/
      ThemeToggle.test.tsx    # Theme cycling, CSS class application

Key requirements:
- Tests must not require a live engine; all API calls mocked via mock-fetch.ts
- Confirm whether test-examples/setup.ts patterns (DOM globalThis setup, selective fetch mock) are needed for this project or if Deno's built-in test runner handles it
- useObservationState tests verify signal state is module-scoped (not re-initialised between imports)
- web/deno.json gets a test task
- deno check web/main.ts passes after all changes

title: create a marketing and docs site for Zephyr
description: We need more than just a GitHub README for the project. The site could be either GitHub pages or a Deno Fresh site hosted with Deno Deploy. If going with Deno Deploy then we need to also choose and register a domain name.

title: Finish /archive route (year/month grid + data downloads)
description: Complete the /archive route (currently a nav stub). The route should display: (1) a grid of available years with data, each year showing summary stats (annual total rain, avg temp range) and expandable or linkable to a monthly breakdown, (2) for each month: summary stats (monthly total rain, avg/high/low temp) and download links for data in formats: NOAA monthly climate summary text, JSON (array of DailyAggregate), CSV (tabular daily data). Add engine API endpoints as needed: GET /api/archive/years (list years with data), GET /api/archive/{year} (monthly summaries for year), GET /api/archive/{year}/{month} (daily data for month). Uses getDailyAggregates() from storage. Downloads serve pre-formatted or on-demand generated content.

title: Fix Header to show observation timestamp, updated on poll
description: The Header component (web/components/layout/Header.tsx) currently shows a datetime that is SSR-rendered and static — it does not update when new observations arrive. The datetime shown should represent the timestamp of the most recently loaded observation (the 'ts' field from the Observation object), not the page load time or current wall clock. Update CurrentConditions island to pass the observation timestamp (from the Signal) to the Header as a prop or via a shared Signal. Header should reactively display the observation timestamp, updating each time the 60-second poll fires. This reflects 'time of data' not 'current time', which is the correct semantic for a weather display.

title: Complete GW1000 LAN API poller response parsing
description: engine/src/ingest/poller.ts fetches the GW1000 LAN API but has a TODO comment for parsing the JSON response into an Observation. Complete the implementation: parse the GW1000 live_data API response (documented in the Ecowitt LAN API PDF), map response fields to Observation + SensorReading[], handle missing/null/undefined sensors gracefully, apply unit conversions (GW1000 LAN API returns imperial units — use existing unit conversion helpers in src/domain/units.ts). The parsed data should go through the same storage path as push ingest. Test with real GW1000 response format.

title: Rename GW1000-specific references to generic driver terminology
description: Throughout the codebase, README files, config, and user-facing text, replace GW1000-specific language with generic driver terminology where appropriate. Changes: README 'Ecowitt GW1000 gateway support' → 'Ecowitt-compatible gateway support (GW1000 is the first supported driver, more to come)', env vars GW_HOST/GW_PORT can stay but should be documented as driver-specific config, all comments referencing 'GW1000 LAN API' → 'driver LAN API', poller.ts class/function names generalised. In all user-facing text, clarify GW1000 is simply the first supported driver.

title: Refactor ingest to plugin-based driver system
description: Restructure engine/src/ingest/ to a plugin-based driver system. Define a Driver interface: { name: string, routes: RouteDefinition[], poller?: PollerDefinition }. RouteDefinition: { method, path, handler: (req, config) => Promise<NormalizedData> }. Each driver lives in engine/src/ingest/drivers/{name}/index.ts. The main push.ts becomes a driver loader that iterates registered drivers and mounts their routes on the HTTP server. Start by extracting current WU and Ecowitt push handlers and GW1000 poller into engine/src/ingest/drivers/gw1000/ as the first driver. The driver loader in main.ts reads config to determine which drivers to activate.

title: [Epic] Plugin-Based Ingest Driver System
description: Refactor the ingest layer from hardcoded WU/Ecowitt handlers to a plugin-based driver architecture. Each driver lives in engine/src/ingest/drivers/{name}/ and implements a standard interface. Drivers register HTTP routes and optional poll capabilities. The GW1000/Ecowitt becomes the first driver. This makes adding new hardware support clean and contributor-friendly without modifying core code.

title: Update API endpoints to accept optional station_id filter
description: All observation API endpoints (/api/observations/latest, /api/observations, /api/observations/range, /api/observations/aggregate, /api/observations/daily, /api/observations/today) should accept an optional ?station= query parameter. Update StorageAdapter interface methods to accept optional stationId?: string parameter. Update SQLite and MySQL adapter implementations to add WHERE station_id = ? clauses when stationId is provided. Update router.ts to pass station_id from query params to storage calls. When no station_id provided and multiple stations exist, return data for the default/first station. Document in API reference.

title: Update ingest to route observations by device ID to station_id
description: Currently engine/src/ingest/push.ts assigns config.station.name as station_id for all observations regardless of which device pushed. With multi-station config, each device push should be routed to the correct station_id using a device registry (push device ID / PASSKEY → station_id mapping in config). Update push.ts WU and Ecowitt handlers to read the device identifier from push params (params.ID for WU, params.PASSKEY for Ecowitt) and look up the correct station_id in the device registry. Fall back to the default station if device not found in registry.

title: Refactor StationConfig to support multiple stations
description: engine/config.ts defines a single StationConfig. Refactor to support an array of station configs, each with a unique id/slug field. Add a device_registry section to config that maps ingest device identifiers (e.g. GW1000 push station ID / Ecowitt PASSKEY / MAC address) to station_id values. Update config loading and validation. Update all code that currently reads config.station to use config.stations[0] as default or look up by station_id. Maintain single-station backward compatibility (if only one station defined, use it as default for all ingest).

title: [Epic] Multi-Station Support
description: Full-stack multi-station support: config allows multiple station definitions, ingest routes observations to the correct station_id based on a device registry, storage queries accept optional station_id filter, and the web UI supports station selection. The SQLite and MySQL storage layers already support multiple station_ids at the data level — this epic covers config, ingest, API, and UI layers.

title: Create install one-liner script
description: Create install.sh at the repo root (also symlinked/served from a stable URL) that can be run as: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cngarrison/zephyr/main/install.sh | bash. Script must: (1) detect OS and architecture (linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64), (2) fetch latest GitHub Release tag via GitHub API, (3) download appropriate compiled binaries and .deb (if on Debian/Ubuntu/RPi) or raw binaries otherwise, (4) install via dpkg or manual copy to /usr/bin/, (5) create 'zephyr' system user if not exists, (6) install systemd unit files, (7) create /etc/zephyr/ and /var/lib/zephyr/ with correct permissions, (8) prompt user for initial config (station name, lat, lon, timezone, ports) and write to /etc/zephyr/engine.env and /etc/zephyr/web.env, (9) systemctl enable+start zephyr.target, (10) print next steps and URLs. Script must be idempotent (safe to re-run for upgrades).

title: Create .deb package build pipeline
description: Create tooling to build Debian .deb packages for Zephyr (engine, web, CLI binaries) for Raspberry Pi OS and Ubuntu/Debian. Use nFPM (https://nfpm.goreleaser.com/) or dpkg-deb with a DEBIAN/control template. Package should: install binaries to /usr/bin/, install systemd unit files to /lib/systemd/system/, install example config to /etc/zephyr/ with correct permissions, create /var/lib/zephyr/ with ownership by 'zephyr' user, run postinst to create system user and enable units, run postrm to clean up on uninstall. Build for linux-aarch64 (RPi) and linux-x86_64. Output .deb files to dist/ alongside compiled binaries.

title: Create docs/ directory with comprehensive guides
description: Create a docs/ directory with the following guides: docs/install.md (all install options: one-liner, distro packages, manual), docs/configure.md (all configuration options, env vars, config file format, /etc/zephyr paths), docs/api.md (complete REST API reference with request/response examples for all engine endpoints), docs/drivers.md (how to write a new ingest driver, using weewx drivers as reference for porting), docs/themes.md (how to create/install a Tailwind-based theme), docs/storage-adapters.md (how to implement StorageAdapter interface), docs/consumers.md (how to write a web consumer other than the built-in Fresh web, using the REST API directly).

title: Add CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Add CONTRIBUTING.md to guide contributors. Cover: development setup (Deno v2.2+, running engine+web locally), code conventions (TypeScript strict, Temporal API, SI units, no hardcoded values), PR process, how to add a new ingest driver, how to add a storage adapter, how to create a theme, AI-assisted contribution encouragement (reference AGENTS.md), issue tracking with Beads bd commands, and where to ask questions.

title: Add LICENSE file (MIT)
description: The README states 'License: MIT' but no LICENSE file exists in the repository root. Add a standard MIT LICENSE file with correct copyright holder and year. Required for the project to be properly open source and for GitHub to recognise the license badge and SPDX identifier.

title: Update web/README.md to reflect current UI
description: web/README.md describes an early version of the web dashboard. Update to reflect: all implemented routes (/, /yesterday, /week, /month, /year, /history, /almanac, /archive), island architecture (CurrentConditions, all chart islands for both raw and aggregate data, ThemeToggle), full component structure (Header, NavTabs, AggregateView, ConditionCard, ChartsGrid, HeatmapTable, etc.), API proxy routes, Vite+Fresh+Tailwind v4 setup, weather-icons vendored in static/, and correct configuration variables (WEB_ENGINE_URL, WEB_PORT).

title: Update engine/README.md to reflect current architecture
description: engine/README.md references the old file structure (sqlite.ts, mysql.ts stub) and is missing features added since it was written. Update to reflect: current storage plugin system (providers/sqlite/, providers/mysql/), complete API endpoint list including /api/almanac, /api/observations/daily, /api/observations/today, /api/observations/aggregate, /api/config, current module structure under src/, correct env var names (DB_PROVIDER), migration script documentation, and accurate ingest driver paths.

title: Update root README.md to reflect current project state
description: The root README.md was written early and is significantly out of date. It references old architecture and missing features that are now complete (almanac, history, aggregate views, today stats, storage plugin system, MySQL adapter, weewx migration, all chart routes). Update to reflect: current architecture (two-daemon Deno workspace), all implemented features, accurate Quick Start, current env var names (DB_PROVIDER not DB_TYPE), accurate API endpoint list (/api/almanac, /api/observations/today, /api/observations/daily, /api/observations/aggregate), correct module structure, and updated planned features. Add sections for: all install options (one-liner, distro packages, manual), driver configuration, storage configuration.

title: Write AGENTS.md with AI contribution guidelines
description: Zephyr wants to encourage AI-assisted contributions. AGENTS.md should provide instructions for AI agents (and humans using AI) working on the codebase. Content must include: project structure overview, coding conventions (Deno/TypeScript strict, Temporal API for all date math, SI units internally, no hardcoded values), how to add a new ingest driver (read existing driver from weewx repo and write Zephyr version), how to update/create a theme, how to implement a storage adapter, key design decisions to preserve, and how to use AI tools effectively when contributing. Should also include sections directly applicable to README files — e.g. how to prompt an AI to add a new feature, update a theme, or port a weewx driver. This file is picked up automatically by AI coding assistants.

title: [Epic] Documentation & Community
description: Group of tasks to establish all project documentation: AGENTS.md for AI contributor guidance, updated README files, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING guide, and a docs/ directory. Goal is to make Zephyr welcoming to contributors and easy to understand for new users and AI coding assistants.

title: Migrate beads issue tracker to community shared dolt server
description: Beads currently uses a local embedded Dolt database (.beads/embeddeddolt). Migrate to the community shared Dolt server to enable collaboration and shared issue visibility for open source contributors. Update .beads/config.yaml with the remote server connection settings. Run 'bd sync' to push existing local issues to the remote. Document the remote server URL and any access requirements in CONTRIBUTING.md so future contributors can sync issues locally.
close_reason: Migrated to GitHub remote (git+ssh://git@github.com/cngarrison/zephyr.git) via bd dolt remote add + bd dolt push. Updated CONTRIBUTING.md with bd bootstrap and sync instructions for contributors.

title: API authentication and key system
description: Add optional API key authentication to the engine REST API. When enabled via config (api.auth_required: true), all /api/* endpoints require a Bearer token in Authorization header or X-API-Key header. Keys are configured as a static list in config.toml (simple, no database required). Ingest endpoints (/ingest/*) have separate handling — use existing station push keys or a separate ingest key config. This allows safely exposing Zephyr to the internet (e.g. for a public weather station website) without leaking private data or allowing unauthorised writes.

title: Static HTML export / embeddable widget for HomeAssistant
description: Add a static HTML export mode generating a self-contained widget suitable for embedding in HomeAssistant dashboards or other home automation UIs. Engine endpoint: GET /api/export/widget returns a standalone HTML snippet with inline CSS and current observation data (no external JS/CSS dependencies). Alternatively a web route /embed/current that renders a minimal SSR widget page suitable for iframe embedding. Widget shows: current temp, humidity, pressure, wind, rain today. Configurable via station config or query params.

title: Cloud upload adapter: Ecowitt cloud
description: Add a cloud upload adapter that forwards observations back to the Ecowitt cloud service. Implement in engine/src/uploaders/ecowitt-cloud.ts. Uses Ecowitt's cloud upload API. Config: ecowitt.application_key, ecowitt.api_key, ecowitt.mac. Runs asynchronously post-ingest. This closes the loop for users who want both local storage (via Zephyr) and Ecowitt cloud backup simultaneously.

title: Cloud upload adapter: CWOP/APRS
description: Add a cloud upload adapter for CWOP (Citizen Weather Observer Program) via APRS-IS protocol. Implement in engine/src/uploaders/cwop.ts. Connects to APRS-IS servers (rotate.aprs2.net:14580) via TCP, authenticates with station callsign and passcode, and submits APRS weather report packets on each observation. Config: cwop.station_id (callsign), cwop.passcode. Format observation as an APRS weather packet (WX format). Runs asynchronously post-ingest.

title: Cloud upload adapter: Weather Underground
description: Add a post-ingest cloud upload adapter that forwards observations to Weather Underground Personal Weather Station network. Implement in engine/src/uploaders/wunderground.ts. Uses the WU PWS upload API (GET https://rtupdate.wunderground.com/weatherstation/updateweatherstation.php with query params). Config: wu.station_id, wu.password (in config.toml). Convert SI units back to imperial for WU API. Uploaders run asynchronously after successful ingest — they must not block the ingest response or throw on failure (log errors only). Enable/disable via config.

title: NOAA-style monthly climate summary text report generation
description: Generate NOAA-format monthly climate summary text reports from daily aggregate data in the daily_aggregates table. NOAA reports include: header with station name/location/month, tabular daily rows (high temp, low temp, avg temp, precipitation, heating degree days, cooling degree days), and a monthly summary row. Engine endpoint: GET /api/reports/noaa?year=YYYY&month=MM returns plain text (Content-Type: text/plain). Used by the /archive route for NOAA format downloads. Can also render in a web route /reports/noaa?year=YYYY&month=MM as a preformatted text page.

title: Replace observation polling with SSE for live updates
description: Currently CurrentConditions island polls /api/observations/latest every 60 seconds via setInterval fetch. Replace with Server-Sent Events (SSE) for efficient real-time push updates. Changes needed: (1) engine: add GET /api/observations/stream SSE endpoint that emits an 'observation' event whenever a new observation is ingested (push or poll triggers the emit), (2) web: add proxy route routes/api/observations/stream.ts, (3) islands/CurrentConditions.tsx: use EventSource instead of setInterval; reconnect automatically on disconnect; fall back to polling if SSE is unavailable. This eliminates unnecessary HTTP round-trips and gives truly live updates (data arrives as it's ingested, not on a fixed 60s clock).

title: Create zephyr CLI binary with cliffy
description: Create cli/main.ts (or engine/cli/main.ts) using cliffy (https://cliffy.io) for command parsing. Initial commands: 'zephyr storage migrate --from weewx --to sqlite' (wraps existing engine/scripts/migrate-weewx.ts), 'zephyr storage info' (show DB stats: record count, date range, storage size), 'zephyr config show' (display parsed current config), 'zephyr config init' (interactive wizard writing config.toml), 'zephyr driver list' (list configured/active drivers), 'zephyr driver add' (interactive driver setup wizard). Compile as standalone binary using deno compile for linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, darwin-aarch64. Add compile task to root deno.json. Include in GitHub release workflow and .deb package as /usr/bin/zephyr.

title: [Epic] Zephyr CLI Tool
description: A compiled standalone CLI binary 'zephyr' using cliffy for command parsing. Provides administrative commands: storage migration, config management, driver management. Compiled as a standalone binary (no Deno needed at runtime) for linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, darwin-aarch64. Included in GitHub releases and .deb package alongside engine and web binaries.

title: Implement theme/skins system (Tailwind-based, compiled-binary-compatible)
description: Add a theme/skins system to the web UI. First version: multiple named Tailwind colour palettes defined as CSS custom property sets in assets/styles.css (e.g. 'default', 'dark-ocean', 'forest', 'high-contrast'). Each theme is a named block of --color-* CSS variable overrides applied to :root or a [data-theme=X] selector. Theme selection persists in localStorage. Since web runs as a compiled binary (Vite-bundled assets baked in), themes must be fully bundled at build time — no external CSS loading at runtime. Extend ThemeToggle island to cycle through or select named themes. Document how to add a new theme in docs/themes.md. Ensure dark/light mode still works within each theme.

title: Update web UI for multi-station selection
description: Add station selector to the web UI when multiple stations are configured. The selector should appear in the Header or NavTabs area. Selecting a station persists the choice in localStorage and as a URL query param (?station=id). All API data fetches include the selected station_id. Update /api/config endpoint to include the list of all configured stations (ids and names) so the web can render the selector. This task depends on multi-station config and API filter tasks being complete.

title: Create PostgreSQL storage adapter
description: Implement a PostgreSQL StorageAdapter in engine/src/storage/providers/postgres/ following the same pattern as the MySQL adapter. Use npm:postgres (Deno-compatible) as the driver. Env vars: POSTGRES_HOST, POSTGRES_PORT, POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DATABASE. Include a forward-only migration runner and initial schema migration matching the MySQL schema adjusted for PostgreSQL syntax (SERIAL or BIGSERIAL for IDs, DOUBLE PRECISION instead of DOUBLE, etc.). Register in factory.ts with DB_PROVIDER=postgres. Very low priority — SQLite and MySQL cover most use cases but PostgreSQL is commonly available in cloud environments.
